meta.title: Planting Calendar by Climate Zone
sources: Bureau of Meteorology Australia climate zones
Standard back-of-seed-packet planting windows (Burpee, Botanical Interests, Johnny's Selected Seeds)
meta.zone_shift_method: Linear month offset based on USDA zone shift (+2 for Zone 3-4, +1 for Zone 5, 0 for Zone 6, -1 for Zone 7-8, -2 for Zone 9-10). Tropical AU uses seasonal wet/dry remapping.

tomato.notes: Warm-season. Transplant after last frost. Indeterminate varieties fruit until first frost.

basil.notes: Frost-tender. Pinch tips to encourage bushy growth.

lettuce.notes: Cool-season. Bolts in heat — provide shade in summer. Sow successively every 2 weeks.

carrot.notes: Direct sow only — transplants fail. Loose, stone-free soil. Thin seedlings to 5cm.

pepper.notes: Long season. Start indoors 8 weeks before last frost. Bell peppers need warm nights.

cucumber.notes: Warm-season. Trellis vines for clean fruit. Needs consistent water.

zucchini.notes: Prolific — 1-2 plants per family. Harvest at 15-20cm for best flavor.

kale.notes: Cold-hardy — frost actually sweetens leaves. Cut-and-come-again.

spinach.notes: Cool-season. Bolts quickly in heat. Spring and fall crops.

beans.notes: Bush and pole types. Direct sow after soil reaches 15°C. Inoculate seeds.

peas.notes: Cool-season. Trellis for vining types. Pick frequently for continuous production.

corn.notes: Plant in blocks (not rows) for pollination. Heat-loving.

garlic.notes: Plant cloves in fall, mulch heavily. Harvest when 1/3 of leaves brown.

onion.notes: Long-day types north of 35° latitude, short-day south. Choose variety for your zone.

strawberry.notes: Plant crowns in early spring or fall. June-bearing varieties fruit once; everbearing all season.

radish.notes: Fast — 25-30 days from sow to harvest. Cool-season. Sow successively.

beet.notes: Direct sow. Thin to 10cm. Both roots and greens are edible.

chard.notes: Heat-tolerant alternative to spinach. Cut outer leaves; plant keeps producing.

parsley.notes: Slow to germinate. Soak seeds overnight. Biennial — flowers second year.

cilantro.notes: Cool-season — bolts fast in heat. Sow successively. Let some flower for coriander seed.

dill.notes: Direct sow only. Self-sows readily. Companion to brassicas and tomatoes.

mint.notes: Vigorous spreader — contain in pots. Perennial. Cut back hard each fall.

thyme.notes: Perennial. Drought-tolerant once established. Cut and dry for winter use.

rosemary.notes: Perennial. Marginal in cold zones — mulch heavily or grow in pots.

marigold.notes: Companion planting — repels nematodes and many pests. Deadhead for continuous bloom.

sunflower.notes: Direct sow. Spacing varies by variety — giants need 60cm. Pollinator magnet.

zinnia.notes: Heat-loving cut flower. Deadhead for blooms until frost.

broccoli.notes: Cool-season. Spring and fall crops. Cut main head then side shoots keep coming.

cauliflower.notes: Cool-season. More finicky than broccoli. Blanch heads by tying leaves over them.

cabbage.notes: Cool-season. Heirloom storage varieties keep through winter.

potato.notes: Plant seed potatoes after last frost. Hill soil up around stems as they grow.

sweet_potato.notes: Needs long warm season. Start slips indoors 6-8 weeks before transplant.

pumpkin.notes: Needs space — 1 vine per 2-3m². Cure for 10 days at 27°C for storage.

watermelon.notes: Heat-loving. Trellis small varieties. Tendril nearest fruit dries when ripe.

eggplant.notes: Heat-loving. Stake or cage — heavy fruit. Glossy skin indicates ripe.

okra.notes: Heat-loving Southern staple. Pick pods young (5-8cm) — they get tough fast.
